content:
Overview

Manual cloud provisioning leads to configuration drift, security 'dark debt,' and non-deterministic environments. This project engineers a secure, version-controlled Landing Zone on GCP that enforces immutability and follows NIST 800-53 security controls from the first line of code.

Modular Design

Encapsulated Terraform Modules

Utilized Terraform Modules to encapsulate VPC networking, IAM service accounts, and Compute Engine resources for maximum reusability and dry-code practices. Each module is deterministic: same inputs produce the same hardened outputs.

Provisioned Through Orchestrated Interfaces

Provisioned environments through consistent module interfaces and plan/apply gates so deployments are orchestrated—not improvised.

Backend State Management

Configured remote state backends with GCS and state-locking to prevent concurrent execution, ensuring a single source of truth and protecting state integrity across multi-user CI runs.

Hardened Network Fabric

Private-First VPC with Controlled Egress

Architected a 'Private-First' VPC with Cloud NAT and VPC Peering. Zero public IP addresses are assigned to compute resources; all egress is controlled and logged.

Least-Privilege Connectivity

Configured routing/peering to enable private administration pathways while enforcing logged, least-privilege access patterns between tiers.

OS Hardening

Integrated automated OS initialization scripts to enforce CIS Benchmarks upon deployment—disabling unnecessary services, configuring SSH keys, and enabling auditd—so security posture is applied at provisioning time.

Challenges & Solution

Secrets & State

Managing sensitive provider credentials and preventing state file corruption in a multi-user environment without leaking secrets into logs or unmanaged workspaces.

Solution: Implemented GCP Secret Manager integration and restricted access to the GCS state bucket using fine-grained IAM roles (Least Privilege), ensuring only authorized CI/CD runners can modify infrastructure.

Compliance & Governance

NIST 800-53 Control Alignment

Aligned with NIST 800-53 (AC, IA, SC). Infrastructure is validated against organizational policies before deployment, so only compliant configuration deltas are provisioned.

Outcomes

<5 minEnvironment setup time
100%CIS Google Cloud Foundations Benchmark compliance
DeterministicDrift elimination via next terraform apply

Before / After

BeforeManual Provisioning with configuration drift and security 'dark debt'.
AfterHardened Landing Zone: Encapsulated, Deterministic, Backend-State Managed, Policy-Validated Infrastructure.
